item.title: Retired and sued for debt? Here's what creditors can (and can't) take
item.description: Creditors can sue you in retirement, but they certainly can't take everything. So, what's protected and what isn't?

item.title: LIRR riders brace for long commutes as strike enters its third day
item.description: LIRR commuters are taking shuttle buses and subways into New York City as the largest commuter rail system in North America remains shut down.

item.title: Iran war "re-escalation risks" keep oil prices volatile as talks stagger on
item.description: Oil prices remain volatile due to Iran war "re-escalation risks," as Tehran says it has shared another set of revised peace deal terms with the U.S.

item.title: Judge rules some key evidence can be used in Luigi Mangione's state trial
item.description: A New York judge ruled Monday on whether evidence found in Luigi Mangione's backpack during his arrest can be used during his state trial.

item.title: Cuba's leader says country poses "no threat" to U.S. after military drone report
item.description: The Trump administration has placed intense pressure on Cuba's communist leadership.

item.title: Georgia readies for key primaries as Trump-backed GOP candidate beats out Sen. Bill Cassidy
item.description: Georgia is set to hold key primaries on Tuesday, including a race between three Republicans challenging incumbent Sen. Jon Ossoff. Meanwhile, Louisiana Sen. Bill Cassidy lost his reelection bid to Rep. Julia Letlow and Louisiana State Treasurer John Fleming, who advanced to a runoff. CBS News' Skyler Henry and Fin Gómez have the latest.

item.title: NextEra Energy to acquire Dominion in a $67 billion utility deal
item.description: The combined company will serve about 10 million utility customers across Florida, Virginia, North Carolina and South Carolina.

item.title: Police kill escaped tiger after it injures man in Germany
item.description: It is believed the tiger was owned by a woman who describes herself as Germany's "Tiger Queen," CBS News partner BBC News reported.

item.title: Can bankruptcy stop Social Security garnishment?
item.description: Bankruptcy can halt many garnishments — but Social Security is a different story. Here's what to know.

item.title: United Nations delegates from Israel and Iran react to diplomatic tensions
item.description: United Nations delegates from Iran and Israel spoke to CBS News' Anna Shecter about the tensions fueled by global conflicts.

item.title: Expert on Cuba's links to Russia, China as report on drone threat emerges
item.description: An Axios report on Cuba acquiring attack drones is adding to concerns about the regime's threats to the U.S. Connor Pfeiffer, a senior director of government relations at FDD Action, joins CBS News with more.

item.title: Ebola outbreak in Congo and Uganda declared global health emergency
item.description: At least 80 deaths have been reported in a new Ebola disease outbreak in Congo and Uganda, authorities said.

item.title: Mother and daughter describe terrifying tornado that leveled newly-built home
item.description: A destructive tornado tore through parts of Nebraska, sending debris flying into the air in Howard County, which is about two hours west of Omaha. A mother and daughter survived in their basement, the only part left of their newly-built home. Rob Marciano reports.

item.title: At least 6 Americans in Congo were exposed to Ebola virus, sources say
item.description: The World Health Organization this week declared the Ebola outbreak in Congo and Uganda a "public health emergency of international concern."

item.title: Teens arrested after string of shootings in Texas
item.description: Three people are in custody after a string of shootings in Austin, Texas. CBS News' Ian Lee reports.

item.title: Judge allows some evidence from Luigi Mangione's backpack for state murder trial
item.description: A New York judge ruled Monday that certain evidence from Luigi Mangione's backpack can be used in his state trial for the alleged murder of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson. CBS News' Lilia Luciano has the latest.

item.title: LIRR workers strike, NYC employees feel impact of interrupted train service
item.description: Long Island Rail Road workers are on strike, causing major disruptions for commuters who work in New York City.

item.title: Trump settles $10 billion lawsuit against IRS over leak of his tax returns
item.description: Mr. Trump had accused the Treasury Department and IRS of unlawfully allowing a government contractor to leak his tax returns and those of his sons and company.

item.title: Supreme Court tells lower courts to take new look at 2 voting rights cases
item.description: At issue in the cases was who can bring lawsuits in federal court to address potential violations of Section 2 of the Voting Rights Act.

item.title: World Health Organization sounds alarm about Ebola outbreak
item.description: An Ebola virus outbreak in Africa is alarming global health officials. CBS News' Dr. Celine Gounder joins with more details.

item.title: Trump escalates Iran threats as poll shows Americans feel stressed about higher prices
item.description: Recent CBS News polling data shows Americans are feeling the strain of higher prices as the Iran war continues to impact oil and gas costs. CBS News' Olivia Rinaldi reports.

item.title: Violent tornadoes rip through central U.S.
item.description: Dangerous weather struck parts of the U.S. over the weekend. Tornadoes tore through homes, causing massive damage. CBS News' Rob Marciano reports from Nebraska.

item.title: Trump talks up trade deals with China, but experts see no big wins for U.S.
item.description: President Trump's trip to China could bolster economic relations, but failed to deliver a breakthrough deal, some trade and energy experts said.

item.title: El Niño could bring "double whammy" of high tide flooding, NOAA warns
item.description: The upcoming El Niño could trigger frequent and widespread flooding in coastal areas around the United States, even when storms aren't actively taking place, scientists warn.

item.title: Republican, Democratic strategists react to latest poll on Trump, economy
item.description: Political strategists Kevin Sheridan and Michael LaRosa join CBS News with their reactions to recent CBS News polling on the U.S. economy and President Trump.

item.title: What are today's mortgage interest rates: May 18, 2026?
item.description: Looking to buy a home or refinance your current one? Here are the mortgage interest rates to know first.

item.title: 2 Americans arrested over stunt at viral baby monkey Punch's zoo in Japan
item.description: Two Americans were arrested in Japan after social media posts showed a person jumping into an enclosure at the zoo where viral baby monkey Punch found fame.

item.title: A youth treatment center promised to help struggling teens. Some say they left with more trauma.
item.description: Youth residential treatment programs promise to help struggling teens develop coping skills and return home to become productive members of their communities. A CBS News investigation looks at whether these programs, which are largely unregulated by the federal government, are helping teens.

item.title: 5/17: Face The Nation
item.description: This week on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," as President Trump returns from his trip to China, U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer and Ambassador Alexander Yui, Taiwan's representative to the U.S., join. Plus, Anthony Salvanto has the latest CBS News polling on the economy.

item.title: The dangers posed by cuts to U.S. foreign aid
item.description: Trump's policies have targeted American foreign aid, and with it the humanitarian groups whose work it funds around the world. "Sunday Morning" looks at the impacts of cutting the U.S. Agency for International Development, or USAID.
